steps.title: Spot the comparison signal
steps.content: The passage transitions from Jones to Dimaline with a specific structure: “Jones is hardly the only Indigenous voice in horror” followed by “Métis author Cherie Dimaline has also written in the genre.” The also signals that both share something related to horror writing.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C correctly identifies that shared element: both are Indigenous authors who have written horror fiction.

Jones is explicitly Blackfeet and a horror writer; Dimaline is explicitly Métis and has “also written in the genre”—horror.

The other choices either misidentify their tribal backgrounds (A and B claim both are Blackfeet when Dimaline is Métis) or fabricate unsupported details (D about disliking horror).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
